title: Payment Service Latency Spike - Memory Leak in TransactionCache
root_cause: Memory leak in payment-service v3.1.0 caused by an unbounded in-memory TransactionCache. The cache stored every processed transaction without TTL or max-size eviction. Under production load (~200 txn/min), the cache grew linearly until the JVM heap was exhausted, triggering GC storms and eventual OOMKill. The bug was in TransactionCache.put() which lacked eviction logic.
resolution: Rolled back payment-service to v3.0.9 (stable). Patched v3.1.1 with a bounded Caffeine cache (maxSize=10000, TTL=5min). Added JVM flag -XX:+HeapDumpOnOutOfMemoryError for future debugging. Added memory growth rate alerting.

timeline.event: Memory usage crosses 75% — first MEM-WARN alert
GC pauses exceeding 500ms — latency spikes begin
Memory at 91% — GC overhead consuming 45% CPU
3 of 4 pods OOMKilled — service effectively down
Rollback complete — service recovered, memory stable

lessons_learned: All in-memory caches must have max-size and TTL constraints
Add heap usage growth rate as a monitored metric with alerting
Include memory profiling step in CI/CD load testing
Code review checklist: verify cache eviction policy for any new cache
